title: ADR-0012: Verify detects orphan rows via CHECK TABLE EXTENDED, not self-built anti-join
description: Record the decision that the Verify phase detects FK-violating (orphan) rows by running CHECK TABLE <t> EXTENDED on each child table that has a foreign key (enumerated via information_schema.REFERENTIAL_CONSTRAINTS), delegating detection to the server, rather than building a self-managed anti-join (Path B). Grounds: context7-confirmed MariaDB does not retro-validate when foreign_key_checks is re-enabled, so Verify needs explicit detection; CHECK TABLE EXTENDED reports per-record FK failures with constraint name. Trade-off: report spans more than FK (index/structural corruption) — accepted for an opt-in --verify phase. Consistent with ADR-0002 (we are not a SQL engine/rewriter).
close_reason: ADR-0012 written to docs/adr/0012-verify-detects-orphans-via-check-table-extended.md; Verify term in CONTEXT.md updated to state the CHECK TABLE EXTENDED mechanism

title: ADR-0011: single pinned connection + reset all three Fast Mode session vars on every exit
description: Lock the Fast Mode ownership boundary and connection lifecycle: restore runs on one pinned db.Conn() (required for autocommit=0/Batch correctness); reset foreign_key_checks/unique_checks/autocommit to 1 in a defer on all exit paths (success/fatal/panic); never depend on the dump footer SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=@OLD_...; Verify re-asserts fk=1 itself. Update CONTEXT.md (Fast Mode, Pre-flight Check, Verify).
notes: ADR-0011 complete. CONTEXT.md updates already incorporated in commit e216f39 (mariadb-restorer-abj work, 2026-07-12): Fast Mode section documents pinned connection + 3-var ownership + defer reset; Verify section documents fk=1 re-assertion; Pre-flight Check section documents single pinned connection requirement and Batch correctness rationale. Issue created 2026-07-11 before restructuring.

value: ADR-0021 (LOCKED): Progress reporting for long restores is byte-based and TTY-adaptive. (1) Percentage = byte_offset / dump_size_bytes. Byte-based, NOT statement-based: dump_size_bytes is known free at startup (Dump Identity, ADR-0019) but total statement count is unknowable without a whole-file pre-scan that breaks constant-memory streaming. statements_done is a secondary counter only, never the percentage basis. (2) Single source of truth: the percentage numerator IS the same byte_offset the Checkpoint Store persists (not a parallel display tally) — the bar is a live readout of the resume point, so what the operator sees is exactly where a crash resumes from; they cannot diverge. A separate display counter would be a divergence bug. (3) TTY-adaptive rendering on stdout via term.IsTerminal (same cross-platform primitive as ADR-0017's stdin gate; Unix IoctlGetTermios, Windows GetConsoleMode, re-confirmed via context7 /golang/term): interactive terminal -> one \r-updated status line (percent, MB/s, ETA) throttled ~1/sec; non-interactive pipe/file/cron -> NO \r (would smear a log file), append-only timestamped progress lines at coarse cadence so tail -f/grep stay useful. --progress=auto|plain|none overrides; auto is default. If dump_size_bytes unknown (non-seekable pipe), percentage degrades to 'unknown' + shows bytes-done/throughput. HONESTY CAVEAT: term.GetSize (terminal-width truncation) was NOT in the context7 result — must be verified separately before relied on; status line kept short to avoid needing it. CONTEXT.md gained a 'Progress' term after the Batch block.

Connection Profile lifecycle: profile is created PASSWORD-LESS. 'profile save' writes only non-secret settings (host/port/user/database) and NEVER touches the Master Passphrase — non-interactive & script-safe. Sealing a password is ALWAYS the separate 'set-password' step (the single command that opens the vault). A profile with no sealed_password is first-class (password comes from run-time Credential Source per ADR-0017). Optional opt-in --with-password may chain save+set-password, but default is password-less. Pure CLI design decision, not a library fact — no context7. Not an ADR (not surprising/hard-to-reverse); recorded in CONTEXT.md Connection Profile term.

Q12 progress reporting: REVISED to Option 3 (live per-batch updates). User feedback: 'sepertinya lebih informatif Opsi 3' - user values increased information over reduced noise. Print progress to stderr after every batch COMMIT (160 lines for 10GB dump vs ~20 for periodic). More verbose but more informative - user sees per-batch throughput, detects hang faster. Format: [HH:MM:SS] bytes/total (%) | statements | elapsed | ETA. TTY-adaptive: \r overwrite for interactive, \n append for non-TTY. Implementation inline in batch loop, no goroutine/ticker. See ADR-0027.

Credential Vault nonce: ACAK crypto/rand 96-bit per seal, TANPA counter, sandari batas GCM 2^32 msg/key. TERKUNCI. Konsekuensi langsung ADR-0014/0015 (bukan ADR terpisah). Fakta context7 (pkg.go.dev/crypto/cipher): NewGCMWithRandomNonce 'generates a random 96-bit nonce... A given key MUST NOT be used to encrypt more than 2^32 messages, to limit the risk of a random nonce collision to negligible levels' — batas ini KHUSUS nonce acak (collision lahir-ulang). TOLAK counter nonce karena: (1) rapuh state-ROLLBACK — vault di-restore dari backup lama sementara counter memori maju, atau dua proses tulis konkuren -> counter mundur -> nonce reuse di bawah KEK sama -> AES-GCM RUNTUH TOTAL (attacker pulihkan auth subkey, palsukan tag apa pun), bukan cuma 1 pesan bocor; (2) semua counter-GCM Go (NewGCMWithXORCounterNonce/NewGCMWithCounterNonce/ForTLS13/ForQUIC/ForHPKE) ada di crypto/internal/fips140/aes/gcm = paket INTERNAL FIPS, BUKAN API publik — tak bisa diimpor tanpa nulis konstruksi GCM sendiri (= crypto-authoring yang dihindari). Volume kita mikroskopis (lusinan seal, di-RESET tiap rotasi passphrase krn KEK baru dari salt baru) jadi 2^32 tak pernah didekati. CATATAN KEJUJURAN: doc TIDAK bilang eksplisit 'pakai random bila tak ada counter andal' — itu penalaran rollback dari sisiku, bukan sitiran. Fallback toolchain: Go>=1.24 NewGCMWithRandomNonce (NonceSize=0, Overhead=28=12nonce+16tag, prepend otomatis); Go<1.24 NewGCM + nonce crypto/rand 12-byte manual, batas 2^32 identik, prepend sendiri.

ADR-0020: restore auto-resumes; NO separate 'resume' verb. Command surface for a data load is exactly two shapes: 'restore <file>' (auto-resume from Checkpoint Store when a matching Dump Identity row exists) and 'restore <file> --restart' (force from byte 0). The resume decision is fully data-driven (store row + fingerprint match/mismatch per ADR-0019), so a verb adds no information and would create a 4th ambiguous meaning. Matches native 'mariadb < dump.sql' instinct (re-run same command). Auto-resume is NEVER silent: plain restore that finds a matching row prints a mandatory notice (dump, % progressed, resume byte, 'use --restart to reload from beginning'), symmetric with ADR-0019 mismatch notice. 'resume' survives only as vocabulary for a phase/behaviour (Resume Batch, resume path), never a command. CONTEXT.md demoted all 'the next resume'/'then resumes' phrasing to behaviour.

ADR-0023: Statement splitting is a state-aware streaming lexer, NOT a naive ;/;\n split. Verified via context7 /mariadb-corporation/mariadb-docs: (1) --extended-insert (multi-row INSERT) is on by default via --opt, so one INSERT can be MBs of data hiding stray ; inside quoted payloads; (2) --routines/--events dump stored programs/events, triggers dumped with table def; (3) DELIMITER is a mariadb command-line CLIENT command, not server SQL, used to change the terminator around stored routines containing ;; (4) a bare ; inside an executable comment /*M!100100 ... */ is a SYNTAX ERROR, so mariadb-dump never emits one there. Consequences: lexer tracks single-quote(\-escape + doubled ''), double-quote, backtick, line-comment(-- #), block-comment(/* */), executable-comment(/*! */ /*M! */), and current delimiter. DELIMITER is INTERPRETED AND CONSUMED, never forwarded to server (tool IS the client for parsing). Everything else forwarded VERBATIM — tool skips/strips nothing, server decides what runs. For boundary detection, executable vs plain comment need not be distinguished (both are regions where ; can't terminate). Lexer correctness is UPSTREAM of checkpoint correctness: a boundary mis-placed inside a literal poisons the Byte Offset resume (ties to ADR-0018/0019). CONTEXT.md terms updated: Statement, Statement Splitter. Rejected: naive split, line-oriented split, forwarding DELIMITER, shelling out to mariadb client (forfeits per-statement/Byte-Offset control), full AST parser (overkill, tool re-sends verbatim).

ADR-0014: Credential Vault Argon2id (golang.org/x/crypto/argon2 IDKey) RFC 9106 §4 OPSI KEDUA — time=3, memory=64 MiB, threads=4, keyLen=32, salt 16-byte crypto/rand. Satu vault-level KEK. Salt+PHC direkam SEKALI di SETTINGS ROW (bukan 'header', bukan per-profil) → default cost bisa dinaikkan tanpa merusak vault lama. Alasan tolak opsi 2 GiB: bersaing dgn buffer streaming & langgar janji constant-memory saat restore 9GB+ — BUKAN soal ukuran RAM host / bukan VM. TERKUNCI.

ADR-0015: setiap seal Credential Vault mengikat AAD = profile-id ‖ vault-id ke AES-256-GCM. profile-id (nama profil) tahan intra-swap; vault-id (UUID crypto/rand di SETTINGS ROW, bukan 'header') tahan cross-store transplant. AAD TIDAK disimpan di baris — direkonstruksi saat open dari identity baris + settings row. Rename profil = re-seal (UPDATE baris). Copy seluruh Data Directory tetap jalan (vault-id ikut). TERKUNCI.

ADR-0017: resolusi kredensial = SATU rantai presedensi tunggal, berlaku sama baik profil punya sealed_password maupun tidak: (1) --password-file [eksplisit, DIREKOMENDASIKAN, file 0600 tak bocor ke ps] > (2) --password/-p [eksplisit tapi tak aman, MariaDB docs: visible di ps/history — dihormati TAPI diberi warning] > (3) sealed_password vault [gated Master Passphrase] > (4) MYSQL_PWD env [ambient, warisi child proc, /proc/pid/environ; MariaDB docs strongly-recommend metode lebih aman] > (5) prompt TTY no-echo (term.ReadPassword, TTY-gated term.IsTerminal). Sumber pertama yang menghasilkan password MENANG. Eksplisit override vault (least-surprise: yg diketik sekarang kalahkan state tersimpan; presedensi != endorsement). Env DI BAWAH vault (passphrase-gated > ambient). Headless tanpa sumber = FAIL-CLOSED, bukan hang. context7-confirmed: /golang/term (IsTerminal/ReadPassword lintas-platform Unix termios+Windows SetConsoleMode), /mariadb-corporation/mariadb-docs (-p visible di ps/history; MYSQL_PWD 'strongly recommend more secure method'). TERKUNCI.

ADR-0018: Checkpoint Store opens with PRAGMA synchronous=FULL. Each per-batch byte_offset checkpoint write must be fsync-durable BEFORE the next batch COMMITs to MariaDB. This keeps the resume replay window at most ONE batch — the precondition the Resume Batch's 1062/1050 one-batch tolerance depends on. synchronous=NORMAL/OFF would report the checkpoint committed while its page sits unfsynced in OS cache; a power loss then rolls byte_offset back multiple batches, resume replays N already-committed batches, Resume Batch tolerates only the FIRST, so batch N+1's 1062 becomes a spurious Fatal killing a healthy restore. Fast Mode is tuned ONLY by Batch size (ADR-0009), never by lowering synchronous or checkpointing less often. modernc.org/sqlite honors standard SQLite durability PRAGMAs (context7 /websites/pkg_go_dev_modernc_org_sqlite). Journal mode DELETE-vs-WAL left at default, NOT part of ADR-0018.

ADR-0025: Escape/quote handling mirrors the dump's in-stream SET sql_mode, NOT a hardcoded rule and NOT the live server's @@sql_mode. Lexer carries TWO bits, both updated by OBSERVING the same SET sql_mode statement: (1) no_backslash_escapes (default false = backslash escapes on, MariaDB default) -- NO_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES flips whether backslash escapes; (2) ansi_quotes (default false = double-quote is a string literal) -- ANSI_QUOTES makes double-quote a quoted identifier instead. Both change where a quoted region closes -> wrong guess poisons resume offset. Under ansi_quotes, double-quote closes by the SAME doubled-quote rule the lexer already runs for backtick identifiers (no new machinery). SET sql_mode is forwarded to server verbatim AND observed (distinct from DELIMITER consumed/not-forwarded per ADR-0023; like SET NAMES forwarded per ADR-0024). Observe-then-scan ordering matters. No --sql-mode flag. context7-confirmed: String Literals (backslash escapes unless NO_BACKSLASH_ESCAPES); Identifier Names + e1149 (double-quote is identifier under ANSI_QUOTES, else string; identifiers escape by doubling the quote, e.g. sys.quote_identifier doubles backticks). HONESTY: context7 never literally states backslash is a literal byte inside an identifier -- that backslash is escape-irrelevant inside identifiers is an INFERENCE from doubling being the sole documented escape mechanism, not a direct quote. Design does not rest on it (region-close = doubled-quote rule).

profile list is read-only, passphrase-free, and leaks no secret. Password column is PRESENCE-ONLY: sealed_password IS NOT NULL -> 'vaulted' else '—'. Never shows length/prefix/any plaintext-derived value (would leak entropy). list touches no KEK so it runs even when the Master Passphrase is forgotten (housekeeping stays alive). list does NOT verify the seal: an AAD/vault-id mismatch (ADR-0015 transplant) is only detectable by attempting Open (needs passphrase), so mismatch detection is deferred to restore time, fail-closed. Recorded in CONTEXT.md Connection Profile term; not an ADR.

Q16 locked: Error classification by execution state (completed vs stopped mid-run), not error type. Exit Code 1 untuk ALL Fatal Errors (timeout, connection, parse, disk, etc.) — restore stopped mid-run, resumable. Exit Code 3 exclusively untuk Deferred Object (restore completed, automated replay path). ADR-0030 formalize classification, ADR-0028 fixed (Exit Code 3→1 for timeout). Rationale: operator automation branch on 'what to do next' (resume vs replay vs inspect), bukan 'why it failed'. Checkpoint-resume make all Fatal Errors recoverable. Simple classification (completed with reservations vs stopped) lebih robust dari error type taxonomy.

ADR-0016 (DITULIS ULANG): password tersimpan INLINE sebagai kolom ciphertext pada baris profil di store SQLite Data Directory — BUKAN file vault JSON terpisah. Model diadopsi dari sibling mariadb-magic (kolom password_ciphertext pd baris connections). Material KDF (salt+PHC+vault-id) di SATU settings row. Satu record = satu sumber kebenaran, tak ada drift/orphan. Delete profil = delete ciphertext atomik. File JSON berversi lama DIBATALKAN (over-engineering). File: docs/adr/0016-saved-passwords-stored-inline-on-profile-row.md

Checkpoint Store is MULTI-ROW keyed by dump_identity — one row per Dump, NOT a single global row. Resolves the old CONTEXT.md contradiction ('one row per restore job' vs 'overwrites the single row'). Each COMMIT is an UPSERT on dump_identity that overwrites THAT dump's row (no per-dump history, just the resume position). Rationale: a crashed dump A must stay resumable even after the operator starts an unrelated 'restore dump-B.sql' — B gets its own row and never clobbers A's resume point. dump_identity already existed in the schema (used to refuse seeking into the wrong byte stream), so keying rows by it is a small natural step. Derived open question: completed rows need cleanup so the store doesn't grow unbounded. Recorded in CONTEXT.md Checkpoint Store term; not an ADR.

Dump Identity fingerprint detection contract (extends ADR-0019): head-prefix+size is deliberately O(1)/cheap so a 9GB+ run pays nothing at startup. Contract stated plainly: DETECTS regeneration and swap (they almost always change size or head bytes); does NOT guarantee detection of a same-size in-place edit (operator hand-fixes one statement mid-file, total byte count unchanged) -> head+size still match -> false-positive match -> tool resumes and Seek()s past the edited region; an edit BEFORE the offset is skipped and the restore 'succeeds' without the fix. This blind spot is ACCEPTED not closed. Rejected: (a) skip-region rolling hash over [0..offset) — would force reading the very bytes Seek() exists to skip, an 8GB resume reads 8GB before first statement, erasing the seek advantage on the largest restores; (b) full-file hash — reads all 9GB+ on every startup incl. first runs that never resume. Operator's conscious escape hatch for the false-positive-match case is --restart. IMPORTANT dual role of --restart: NOT the gate for a mismatch (that restart is automatic per ADR-0019); its SOLE purpose is the false-positive match the fingerprint cannot see ('I edited this file, discard checkpoint and reload even though fingerprint matches').

profile delete is a METADATA operation — never requires the Master Passphrase. Rationale: you don't need to OPEN a secret to discard it; deleting undecrypted ciphertext is safe (key never on disk, ADR-0007). Requiring passphrase would conflate 'authorize row delete' with 'ability to decrypt' and turn a profile whose passphrase is lost into an undeletable zombie. Delete drops the row + its sealed_password in one DELETE (no orphan ciphertext). Only gate: interactive y/N confirmation, or --yes for unattended. Recorded in CONTEXT.md Connection Profile term; not an ADR.

profile set-password write-path mirrors ADR-0017 read-path sources: default no-echo prompt entered TWICE to confirm (typo in a sealed password is unrecoverable), unattended via --password-file (0600) or --stdin (cat p | ... --stdin), and --password/-p inline honored-but-warned about ps/history exposure (same MariaDB /mariadb-corporation/mariadb-docs fact). Not a separate ADR — recorded as the write-path section of ADR-0017. One mental model for read and write.

Q10 (lexer state on resume): Checkpoint Store menyimpan 4 kolom lexer state — no_backslash_escapes BOOLEAN, ansi_quotes BOOLEAN, current_delimiter TEXT, charset TEXT — selain byte_offset dan statements_done. Ini guarantee correct boundary detection saat resume: tool restore tidak hanya WHERE (byte offset) tapi HOW TO PARSE (lexer state at that byte). Overhead ~32 bytes per checkpoint negligible untuk per-Batch granularity. Alasan: Context7 tidak konfirmasi mariadb-dump guarantees all SET statements in header, jadi assume header-only adalah fragile; scan from byte 0 adalah correct tapi defeats fast resume. Save state in checkpoint adalah only approach yang both correct dan fast. ADR-0026, CONTEXT.md lines 101-119 updated.

Q14 timeout configuration: LOCKED to Option B (explicit DSN timeouts). Configured timeout=30s (connection establishment, matching MariaDB Connector/J default), readTimeout=5m (I/O read, allows slow server), writeTimeout=10m (I/O write, allows large batch COMMIT ~64MB). Aligns with fail-fast principle (ADR-0006) - MySQL default --connect-timeout is 43200s (12 hours), too permissive. Context7 research: go-mysql-org/go-mysql driver supports timeout/readTimeout/writeTimeout DSN parameters, MariaDB connectors use explicit timeouts (Python: connect_timeout=5.0, Java: 30s default). If timeout reached, driver returns error, tool exits Exit Code 3, user diagnoses and resumes. User can override via flags --timeout/--read-timeout/--write-timeout. Trade-off: if batch COMMIT needs >10min, tool times out - signal that batch too large or server under-resourced. See ADR-0028.

Q15: sql_log_bin adalah dump-owned session variable (passed through verbatim), bukan tool-owned. Tool tidak query @@sql_log_bin, tidak set, tidak warn. Requires BINLOG ADMIN privilege yang restore user mungkin tidak punya. Replication implications (binlog ON = operations replicate downstream, binlog OFF = tidak replicate) adalah operator's decision based on their infra topology. ADR-0029, CONTEXT.md SQL_LOG_BIN term added.

ADR-0022: Interrupt handling during a long restore. First os.Interrupt (Ctrl-C; also SIGTERM on Unix) = graceful drain: finish in-flight batch through COMMIT + checkpoint write, print resume notice, exit clean with byte_offset on a batch boundary (zero replay next run). Second signal = abort immediately onto the crash-resume path (replay <=1 batch, absorbed by Resume Batch, never unsafe). VERIFIED via context7 /golang/go (os/signal): NotifyContext cancels ctx on first signal but 'Future interrupts received will not trigger the default (exit) behavior until the returned stop function is called' -- so bare NotifyContext would SWALLOW the operator's second Ctrl-C; its stop only 'may restore the default behavior' (platform-conditional, too weak for portable Linux+Windows). Mechanism therefore = explicit counting signal.Notify handler where the 2nd delivery calls os.Exit directly, NOT bare NotifyContext. Exit codes: shell 128+signo convention -> 130 (SIGINT), 143 (SIGTERM), distinct from Fatal 1, so scripts tell operator-stop from restore-failure. This is a shell/waitstatus (bash) convention, NOT a Go stdlib guarantee. Cross-platform contract rests on os.Interrupt; SIGTERM is Unix-only, not claimed equivalent on Windows. Batch loop checks cancellation only at safe boundary (between batches, never mid-COMMIT). CONTEXT.md gained an Interrupt term and the Exit Code term gained 130/143.

ADR-0024: lexer scans raw bytes (\[\]byte), not decoded runes. Safe because all structural tokens are ASCII (<0x80) and no UTF-8 multibyte byte is <0x80, so byte-scan can't mistake a multibyte fragment for a delimiter -> no encoding detection, no EEncodingError-retry (unlike HeidiSQL). Hole (ASCII-incompatible utf16/utf16le/ucs2/utf32) closed by MariaDB itself: context7 (SET NAMES, character_set_client) confirms those charsets are NOT valid client charsets, so mariadb-dump's on-by-default SET NAMES makes such a dump non-restorable by ANY client -> out of valid-input set, not an unhandled case. No --encoding flag, no BOM sniffer. Statements forwarded verbatim; server decodes per dump's SET NAMES.

Checkpoint Store row is DELETEd on full successful restore completion — store holds only unfinished restores, size bounded by count of pending restores (normally 0). resume semantics: row present = resumable; row absent = 'nothing to resume, already completed or never started' (distinguished by whether tool has seen that dump_identity). Crash in window after final COMMIT before DELETE leaves benign idle row with byte_offset=EOF; next resume seeks EOF, finds no work, deletes row — cleanup is idempotent. Multi-row keyed by dump_identity.

ADR-0019: Dump Identity (head-prefix+size fingerprint) is the resume session key with exactly TWO outcomes, never a third seek-anyway. match -> resume from stored byte_offset. mismatch (same path, different content: operator regenerated/swapped the dump) -> AUTOMATICALLY restart from byte 0, delete the old dump_identity row, and ALWAYS print a notice (path, % progressed on discarded checkpoint, 'restarting from beginning; old checkpoint discarded'). No --restart flag required: the orphaned checkpoint protects nothing because the old file is gone; awareness is served by the mandatory notice, not a blocking flag. NEVER force-seek a stale offset (seeking into a different byte stream = silent corruption). Restart-onto-dirty-target is safe on the default path because mariadb-dump --opt (ON by default) includes --add-drop-table -> DROP TABLE IF EXISTS before each CREATE TABLE (confirmed context7 /mariadb-corporation/mariadb-docs). Exception: --no-create-info/--skip-opt data-only dumps carry no DROP; reloading onto existing rows hits 1062 on the first fresh INSERT (Resume Batch tolerance does NOT apply to fresh batches) and fails loudly -> operator's concern, not silent merge. Full-file hash to catch same-size in-place edits is out of scope (separate startup-cost trade-off); undetected edit degrades to clean restart, never mid-stream seek.

ADR-0011: restore runs on ONE pinned connection via db.Conn(ctx) — required first for Batch correctness (autocommit=0 only applies to the same connection, Go database/sql pools connections). Tool OWNS the 3 Fast Mode session vars (foreign_key_checks, unique_checks, autocommit): sets them in Pre-flight, resets ALL THREE to 1 via defer on every exit path (clean/Fatal/panic) before connection returns to pool. Never depends on dump footer SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=@OLD_ (only runs on clean finish). Verify re-asserts foreign_key_checks=1 itself before checks. Reset is NOT cosmetic 'don't disturb other clients' (MariaDB session isolation already guarantees that) — it exists because our own process pool may reuse the fk=0 connection.

ADR-0012: Verify detects FK orphan rows via CHECK TABLE <t> EXTENDED per child table that has an FK (enumerated from information_schema.REFERENTIAL_CONSTRAINTS), delegating detection to the server — NOT a self-built anti-join (Path B rejected: would force us to author composite-key + MATCH SIMPLE NULL semantics + schema-qualified LEFT JOIN anti-joins, the SQL-authoring role ADR-0002 keeps us out of). context7-confirmed: re-enabling foreign_key_checks=1 does NOT retro-validate loaded rows, so Verify MUST actively scan; CHECK TABLE EXTENDED reports FK violations per-record with constraint name (Key: t2_ibfk_1, record: '2'). Accepted trade-off: EXTENDED is a full re-scan so Verify also surfaces index/structural corruption, not only FK orphans — fine for an opt-in --verify soundness phase. Verify runs on the pinned connection (ADR-0011), re-asserts fk=1 itself before scanning. Off by default (full scan per FK table is expensive).

profile save is an UPSERT — no separate 'edit' verb. Re-running 'profile save <name>' on an existing name updates only the fields given, keeps the rest, and leaves sealed_password UNTOUCHED (updating host/port does not invalidate the vaulted password; AAD binds on name, not destination — ADR-0015). Idempotent = script/cron friendly (converges to requested state). Trade-off: no 'already exists' guard to catch a mistyped name, mitigated by printing 'created' vs 'updated'. Recorded in CONTEXT.md Connection Profile term; not an ADR.
